title: A neighbor I'll never forget
summary: Describe one neighbor from your past and why you still remember them.
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences about a neighbor you'll never forget. Describe who they were and one memory. Use past simple.
hintStarters: When I was about ten years old, a woman named…
She lived just across the hall, and she always…
I never forgot the day when she…

hintVocab.meaningVi: bình thường, không có gì đặc biệt

sampleText: When I was about nine years old, my family lived in a block of flats in Thessaloniki, in Greece. Our neighbor across the hall was a retired schoolteacher named Mrs. Papadaki. She was a small, quiet woman who lived alone after her husband died. Every afternoon, she sat in her doorway and read thick paperback books with her glasses on the end of her nose. At first, I thought she was unfriendly because she never smiled when she passed us in the corridor. One afternoon, I knocked on her door because I needed a pencil for my homework. She opened the door, looked at me for a long moment, and then walked back inside without a word. I thought she was going to close the door. Instead, she came back with three pencils, a small notebook, and a piece of chocolate. She put them all in my hand and said, 'A student always needs a notebook.' After that day, I knocked on her door every few weeks, and she always answered. She lent me my first English dictionary when I was eleven. My family moved away two years later, and I left the dictionary behind by mistake. I still think about that book sometimes.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: lived alone after her husband died
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: sống một mình sau khi chồng qua đời

sampleAnnotations.phrase: At first, I thought she was unfriendly
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Ban đầu, tôi nghĩ bà ấy không thân thiện
Thay vào đó, bà ấy quay lại với

sampleAnnotations.phrase: A student always needs a notebook
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Một học sinh luôn cần một quyển vở

sampleAnnotations.phrase: left the dictionary behind by mistake
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: để quên quyển từ điển lại do nhầm lẫn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I still think about that book sometimes
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Đôi khi tôi vẫn nghĩ về quyển sách đó

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for a complete story from the past
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Tất cả các động từ chính trong câu chuyện (lived, sat, knocked, opened, came, put, said, lent, moved, left) đều dùng quá khứ đơn vì các sự việc đã kết thúc hoàn toàn.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: All main verbs in the story (lived, sat, knocked, opened, came, put, said, lent, moved, left) use past simple because every event is finished.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'At first' and 'Instead' to show a change
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'At first, I thought…' giới thiệu ấn tượng ban đầu. 'Instead' cho thấy điều ngược lại hoặc bất ngờ xảy ra. Hai từ này cùng nhau tạo nên bước ngoặt trong câu chuyện.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'At first, I thought…' introduces the first impression. 'Instead' shows that the opposite or something unexpected happened. Together, they create the turning point of the story.

sampleTranslationVi: Khi tôi khoảng chín tuổi, gia đình tôi sống trong một khu chung cư ở Thessaloniki, Hy Lạp. Người hàng xóm đối diện chúng tôi là một giáo viên đã nghỉ hưu tên là bà Papadaki. Bà là một người phụ nữ nhỏ bé, điềm tĩnh, sống một mình sau khi chồng qua đời. Mỗi buổi chiều, bà ngồi ở cửa nhà và đọc những cuốn sách bìa mềm dày với kính đeo trên đầu mũi. Ban đầu, tôi nghĩ bà không thân thiện vì bà không bao giờ mỉm cười khi đi qua chúng tôi trong hành lang. Một buổi chiều, tôi gõ cửa nhà bà vì cần một cây bút chì để làm bài tập về nhà. Bà mở cửa, nhìn tôi một lúc lâu, rồi quay vào trong mà không nói một lời. Tôi nghĩ bà sắp đóng cửa lại. Thay vào đó, bà quay lại với ba cây bút chì, một quyển vở nhỏ, và một miếng sô-cô-la. Bà đặt tất cả vào tay tôi và nói, 'Một học sinh luôn cần một quyển vở.' Sau ngày đó, tôi gõ cửa nhà bà vài tuần một lần, và bà luôn trả lời. Bà cho tôi mượn quyển từ điển tiếng Anh đầu tiên của tôi khi tôi mười một tuổi. Gia đình tôi chuyển đi hai năm sau, và tôi đã vô tình để quên quyển từ điển lại. Đôi khi tôi vẫn nghĩ về quyển sách đó.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural opening sentence for a childhood memory.
options: When I am about nine years old, my family lives in a block of flats.
When I was about nine years old, my family lived in a block of flats.
When I was about nine years old, my family is living in a block of flats.
When I was about nine years old, my family have lived in a block of flats.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple in both the time clause ('was') and the main clause ('lived') is correct for a finished past situation.
mcQuestions.hint: Both verbs in a past-time clause should be past simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase correctly means that she lived alone because her husband was dead?
options: She lived alone after her husband died.
She lived alone after her husband was dying.
She lived alone after her husband has died.
She lived alone after her husband is dead.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'After her husband died' uses past simple in the time clause, matching the past simple in the main clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Past simple follows 'after' in a finished past situation.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that shows the narrator's first (wrong) impression.
options: At first, I thought she was unfriendly.
At first, I think she is unfriendly.
At first, I am thinking she was unfriendly.
At first, I thought she is unfriendly.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'At first, I thought she was unfriendly' uses past simple for both verbs — the thinking and the quality observed.
mcQuestions.hint: Past simple in both verbs — the opinion and the quality.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct use of 'Instead' to show the opposite happened.
options: I thought she was going to close the door. Instead, she came back with pencils.
I thought she was going to close the door. Instead she come back with pencils.
I thought she was going to close the door, instead she came back with pencils.
Instead I thought she was going to close the door, she came back with pencils.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Instead' starts a new sentence with a comma after it. It shows that the opposite or something unexpected happened.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Instead,' at the start of a new sentence + comma shows a contrast.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means 'to allow someone to use something and then return it'?
options: borrow
lend
give
leave behind
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Lend' means to give something to someone for a period of time. 'Borrow' is the action from the receiver's point of view.
mcQuestions.hint: If I give you my book for a week, I ___ it to you.

mcQuestions.prompt: Select the sentence where 'without a word' is used correctly.
options: She walked back inside without a word.
She walked back inside without words.
She walked back inside without any word.
She walked back inside with no any word.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Without a word' is the fixed expression meaning 'saying nothing'. It uses the singular 'a word'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option is the fixed phrase for 'saying nothing at all'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes a building with many flats in it?
options: a block of flats
a row of houses
a row of flats
a block of houses
mcQuestions.explanation: 'A block of flats' is the standard British English term for a building that contains many individual apartments.
mcQuestions.hint: Which phrase means a large building with many apartments?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that correctly says the narrator forgot to take the dictionary.
options: I left the dictionary behind by mistake.
I left behind the dictionary by mistake.
I leave the dictionary behind by mistake.
I left the dictionary behind on purpose.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Left the dictionary behind' means forgot to take it. 'By mistake' shows it was accidental, not deliberate.
mcQuestions.hint: Which sentence uses past simple and 'by mistake' to show it was accidental?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that uses 'knocked on' correctly.
options: I knocked on her door because I needed a pencil.
I knocked her door because I needed a pencil.
I knock on her door because I need a pencil.
I was knocked on her door because I needed a pencil.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Knock on the door' is the correct collocation. Past simple 'knocked' fits the finished past event.
mcQuestions.hint: The verb is 'knock on', not 'knock'. Which tense fits?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes the neighbor's final impression on the narrator?
options: I still think about that book sometimes.
I always think about that book every day.
I never thought about that book again.
I sometimes am thinking about that book.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I still think about that book sometimes' uses present simple + 'still' and 'sometimes', showing an ongoing occasional memory.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option uses present simple and 'still' to show a lasting memory?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase means a person who has stopped working because of their age?
options: curious
ordinary
retired
patient
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Retired' describes someone who has stopped working, usually at an older age.
mcQuestions.hint: Which word describes someone who no longer works because they are old enough to stop?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Have you ever misjudged someone when you first met them, only to discover they were very different from what you expected? What changed your mind?
